title: Camp Hanuman Temple
tagline: Camp Hanuman Temple — spiritual town of Gujarat

overview.short: The Shree Camp Hanuman Temple (Hindi: कैंप हनुमान मंदिर) is one of the biggest Hanuman temples in India.
overview.description: The Shree Camp Hanuman Temple (Hindi: कैंप हनुमान मंदिर) is one of the biggest Hanuman temples in India. It is situated in Ahmedabad Cantonment in Shahibaug, Ahmedabad in the Indian state of Gujarat. The temple is owned by Shri Hanumanji Mandir Camp Trust Ahmedabad.

nearestAirport.name: Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel International Airport, Ahmedabad

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: AEC metro station
topPlaces.description: AEC is an elevated metro station on the North-South Corridor of the Red Line of Ahmedabad Metro in Ahmedabad, India. Around this metro station holds the main and upcoming Sabarmati HSR Terminal railway station along with AEC Garden Park and Torrent Power Limited.

topPlaces.name: Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel National Memorial
topPlaces.description: The Moti Shahi Mahal is a palace built by the Mughal emperor Shahjahan between 1616 and 1623, when he was still a prince. It hosts the Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel National Memorial, a museum and exhibition centre dedicated to Vallabhbhai Patel in Shahibaug, Ahmedabad, Gujarat, near Civil Hospital, Ahmedabad.

topPlaces.name: Shahibaug
topPlaces.description: Shahibaug is a neighborhood in the city of Ahmedabad, India. It is primarily inhabited by the Marwadi and Jain communities, while the Dudheshwar area in the west of Shahibaug has sizeable populations of Muslims and migrants from other states.

topPlaces.name: Air India Flight 171
topPlaces.description: Air India Flight 171 was a scheduled international passenger flight from Ahmedabad Airport, Gujarat, India, to London Gatwick Airport in Crawley, West Sussex, England. On 12 June 2025, at 13:39 IST (08:09 UTC), the Boeing 787-8 Dreamliner operating the flight crashed 32 seconds after takeoff into the student hostels of the Byramjee Jeejeebhoy Medical College, 1.7 kilometres (1 mi; 0.9 nmi) from the runway.

topPlaces.name: Subhash Bridge
topPlaces.description: Subhash Bridge is a beam bridge over the Sabarmati river in Ahmedabad, Gujarat, India, It was built in 1973 and demolished in 2026.

topPlaces.name: Miya Khan Chishti's Mosque
topPlaces.description: The Miya Khan Chishti's Mosque, or more correctly, the Miya Khan Chishti's Mosque and Tomb, is a Sufi mosque and dargah complex, located on the bank of Sabarmati River in the Shahibaug area of Ahmedabad, in the state of Gujarat, India.

topPlaces.name: Villa Sarabhai
topPlaces.description: Villa Sarabhai, or Villa de Madame Manorama Sarabhai, is a modernist villa located in Ahmedabad, India. Designed by the Franco-Swiss architect Le Corbusier, it was built between 1951 and 1955.

topPlaces.name: Calico Museum of Textiles
topPlaces.description: The Calico Museum of Textiles is located in the city of Ahmedabad in the state of Gujarat in western India. The museum is managed by the Sarabhai Foundation.

items.note: AEC is an elevated metro station on the North-South Corridor of the Red Line of Ahmedabad Metro in Ahmedabad, India. Around this metro station holds the main and upcoming Sabarmati HSR Terminal railway station along with AEC Garden Park and Torrent Power Limited.

The Moti Shahi Mahal is a palace built by the Mughal emperor Shahjahan between 1616 and 1623, when he was still a prince. It hosts the Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel National Memorial, a museum and exhibition centre dedicated to Vallabhbhai Patel in Shahibaug, Ahmedabad, Gujarat, near Civil Hospital, Ahmedabad.

Shahibaug is a neighborhood in the city of Ahmedabad, India. It is primarily inhabited by the Marwadi and Jain communities, while the Dudheshwar area in the west of Shahibaug has sizeable populations of Muslims and migrants from other states.

Air India Flight 171 was a scheduled international passenger flight from Ahmedabad Airport, Gujarat, India, to London Gatwick Airport in Crawley, West Sussex, England. On 12 June 2025, at 13:39 IST (08:09 UTC), the Boeing 787-8 Dreamliner operating the flight crashed 32 seconds after takeoff into the student hostels of the Byramjee Jeejeebhoy Medical College, 1.7 kilometres (1 mi; 0.9 nmi) from the runway.
Subhash Bridge is a beam bridge over the Sabarmati river in Ahmedabad, Gujarat, India, It was built in 1973 and demolished in 2026.

The Miya Khan Chishti's Mosque, or more correctly, the Miya Khan Chishti's Mosque and Tomb, is a Sufi mosque and dargah complex, located on the bank of Sabarmati River in the Shahibaug area of Ahmedabad, in the state of Gujarat, India.
Villa Sarabhai, or Villa de Madame Manorama Sarabhai, is a modernist villa located in Ahmedabad, India. Designed by the Franco-Swiss architect Le Corbusier, it was built between 1951 and 1955.
The Calico Museum of Textiles is located in the city of Ahmedabad in the state of Gujarat in western India. The museum is managed by the Sarabhai Foundation.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Camp Hanuman Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Camp Hanuman Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Camp Hanuman Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel International Airport, Ahmedabad (3 km) and the nearest railway station is Ahmedabad Junction (5 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Camp Hanuman Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Camp Hanuman Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Camp Hanuman Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Camp Hanuman Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Camp Hanuman Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are AEC metro station, Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel National Memorial, Shahibaug, Air India Flight 171, Subhash Bridge.
